briefs.title: Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw amended — updated penalty rules now in force
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has given final adoption to Amendment No. 2, 2026 of the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, updating the framework governing how bylaw notices and penalties are issued and enforced.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to confirm your business remains compliant with any revised penalty provisions.

briefs.title: 190-Signature Petition Filed Against Rezoning Application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council received a resident petition with 190 signatures opposing rezoning application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street. The petition has been noted for information; no decision has been reported.
briefs.action_required: Monitor council proceedings for REZ #25-13 decisions if your business is near or affected by 5058 Imperial Street.

briefs.title: Burnaby Parking Meter and EV Charging Meter Bylaw 14805 receives final adoption
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has given final adoption to Bylaw 14805, amending the Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019. The amendment is now in force.
briefs.action_required: Review updated parking meter and EV charging meter rules to ensure your business operations remain compliant.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw Amendment No. 2, 2026 adopted
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has given final adoption to Amendment No. 2, 2026 of the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, meaning updated municipal fees and charges are now in effect.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to identify any fee changes affecting your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 6800 Lougheed Highway — first and second reading passed
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed first and second reading of Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 5, 2026 for rezoning application REZ #25-18 at 6800 Lougheed Highway.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for third reading and adoption if your business or property is near 6800 Lougheed Hwy.

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezoning at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue Passes First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council gave first and second reading to Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 6, 2026 for rezoning application REZ #25-20 at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The bylaw must still pass third reading and adoption before taking effect.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for third reading and adoption of Bylaw 14804 if you have interests at or near 2209–2225 Springer Avenue.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council advances rezoning of 5058 Imperial Street through second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed first and second reading of Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 4, 2026 for REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street, with four members opposed. The rezoning must still clear further readings before taking effect.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for third reading and adoption of REZ #25-13 if this property affects your operations.

briefs.title: City 2025 Investment Program results and 2026 forecast reported to Council
briefs.summary: Council received the Financial Management Committee's report on the city's 2025 Investment Program results and 2026 investment forecast for information purposes.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan formally initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received for information the project initiation of the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan, signalling the start of translating the long-term community vision into concrete actions.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Development at 6535 Kingsway Passes First Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed preparation of a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-09) to allow a 12-storey mixed-use building with 290 residential units and ground-floor commercial at 6535 Kingsway.
briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming public hearing and bylaw readings if your business or property interests are near 6535 Kingsway.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue passes first reading — no public hearing required
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council directed preparation of a rezoning bylaw for REZ #25-21, allowing a five-storey enclosed self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing is required as the proposal aligns with the Official Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council's agenda for the rezoning bylaw's final readings if this development affects your property or business plans.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy endorsed; implementation plan with community partners authorized
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy and authorized staff to develop an implementation plan with community partners. The Mayor will advocate to the province for renewed UBCM funding.

briefs.title: High-density mixed-use rezoning application accepted for Carrigan Court development
briefs.summary: Council has authorized staff to accept a rezoning application for 3856 & 3870 Carrigan Court, advancing plans for a high-density commercial/residential strata and rental development.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council proceedings for public hearing dates if your business may be affected by this development.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 3900 Grand Promenade rezoning amended to add 362 strata units and hotel/restaurant use
briefs.summary: Council directed preparation of a rezoning bylaw for REZ #24-23, amending a previously approved mixed-use development to replace some office space with hotel/restaurant uses, add 362 market strata units, and transfer inclusionary housing obligations to the Carrigan site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning bylaw progress if your business is affected by development at 3900 Grand Promenade.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Council awards hired equipment services contracts to 19 contractors on quantity-based payment terms
briefs.summary: Council has approved contract awards to 19 separate contractors for hired equipment services, with final payment determined by actual quantities delivered rather than fixed amounts.
briefs.action_required: If you provide hired equipment services, confirm your contract terms and quantity-tracking requirements with the council.

briefs.title: Municipal asphalt paving contract renewed with Blackrete Paving for $1,155,000
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving services for the Engineering Department, valued at an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: If you are a paving or civil contractor, monitor future municipal tender opportunities as this contract is now awarded.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Housing Contract at 8315 14th Avenue Sent Back for Efficiency Review
briefs.summary: Council has referred the contract award for a modular four-plex pilot housing unit back to staff for further review to ensure project management efficiencies before proceeding.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: Council has awarded a $4.2M contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. for the supply and delivery of water metering technology, including taxes.

briefs.title: Municipal council approves $36,250.57 UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Council has approved payment of the Union of BC Municipalities 2026 membership dues totalling $36,250.57 including taxes. This is a routine municipal administrative expenditure with no direct regulatory impact on businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026–2030 Strategic Plan approved by Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's five-year strategic plan (2026–2030), setting the direction for social and affordable housing development in the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the BHA 2026–2030 Strategic Plan to identify development or partnership opportunities relevant to your business.
